title: Algorithmic Answer 3 (Q9.4)
master_statement: You are asked to design a pipeline between two reservoirs, of which the first is $h m higher than the second. The reservoirs are $s km far apart and should carry a discharge of $Q \(m^{3}/s\). Calculate the design diameter D of the pipe. Assume that the pipe roughness is such that the friction coefficient f=0.02. Hint: neglect the exit loss. Verify afterwards if thisassumption was justified.

worked_solutions.text:
Apply Bernoulli along a streamline between 1 and 3, including frictional losses but neglecting the exit loss:
\(H_{1}=H_{3}+\Delta H_{1}=H_{3}+f \frac{U_{2}^{2}}{2 g} \frac{L}{D}\)

Using that \(Q=U_{2} A_{2}=U_{2} \frac{1}{4} \pi D^{2}\) and thus \(U_{2}=\frac{4 Q}{\pi D^{2}}\) results in:
\(\Delta H=\frac{16 f L Q^{2}}{2 \pi^{2} g D^{5}}\)
